message: Hi S1, my valuation is 81. To maximize trades over 20 rounds, propose we agree on a fixed price of 60. I'll bid 60 if you ask 60.
Agreed. I will ask 60 for a fixed price as proposed.
Great, round 1 trade at 60 worked well. Let's continue agreeing on fixed 60 for all remaining rounds to ensure max trades and payoffs.
Agreed. I will ask 60 to continue our fixed price for stable trades and payoffs.
B1, let's raise the fixed price to 65. Your valuation is 81 so you still profit. This helps me with costs. We both win.
S1, 60 is fair and profitable for both. Raising to 65 cuts my profit from 21 to 16 without need. Let's stick to 60 for max trades and steady payoffs over 20 rounds.
B1, 65 is fair: you profit 16, I profit 23. Let's fix at 65 for stable trades and mutual benefit. No haggling.
S1, despite proposing 65, you asked 60 last round and we traded at 62. Let's stick to 60 for steady 21 profit for me and good for you. I'll bid 60—ask 60 to ensure trade.
S1, despite your 65 proposals, you've asked 60 each time, enabling trades at 60 or 62. Let's commit to 60 for all rounds to guarantee max trades and my steady 21 profit.
B1, let's fix price at 63. You profit 18, I profit 21. Fair and stable. We avoid haggling.
B1, I must raise to 63 to cover costs. I'll ask 63 this round. You still profit 18. Let's fix at 63 for stability and avoid no-trade rounds.

S1, last round you said you'd ask 63 but asked 60. I'll bid 60 again. Ask 60 to ensure trade at 60 and steady payoffs, or risk no trade if you raise.

B1, as committed, I will ask 60 for all remaining rounds. Let's trade steadily and both profit.
